core_mechanism: minimum-distance equivalence test: quantify the discrepancy between observed data and a parametric family by minimizing a distance functional over the parameter space, then test whether that minimized discrepancy falls below a preset tolerance (an inversion of the usual point-null test, so rejection certifies approximate agreement rather than exact fit), with reference thresholds obtained from an asymptotic approximation or bootstrap resampling
brief_reason: The abstract states the test statistic is constructed via the minimum distance method and frames the procedure as an equivalence test establishing sufficiently good agreement of data with the model, with critical values from an asymptotic approximation or bootstrapping.
